title: Intensely Smoky Brazilian Lentil Bake
description: A yeasty and juicy Brazilian dish featuring brown lentils, baked in a wood-fired oven and wrapped in banana leaves, perfect for meal prep and macrobiotic diets.
instructions: Preheat the wood-fired oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Soak the brown lentils in water for at least 30 minutes, then drain and set aside.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the chopped onion until softened.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Stir in the smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked lentils, sauteed onion mixture, and nutritional yeast, and stir until well combined.
Layout the banana leaves and divide the lentil mixture among them, spooning it into the center of each leaf.
Fold the banana leaves over the filling to form a tight packet, and secure with kitchen twine if needed.
Place the packets in the preheated oven and bake for 30-40 minutes, until the lentils are tender and the banana leaves are slightly charred.
